h2h_summary: The snippets reveal at least 8 historical meetings between Spain and Austria, including competitive fixtures in the FIFA World Cup and UEFA European Championship, but the complete all-time win-draw-loss totals are truncated and therefore unknown.
key_players_away: David Alaba (central defender, captain, Real Madrid)
Marko Arnautovic (striker, record goalscorer with 47 goals in 132 caps)
team_style_home: Spain play a fluid 4-3-3 under Luis de la Fuente featuring elite ball circulation through Rodri and Pedri in midfield, dangerous wide isolation via Yamal and Williams, and high-pressing coordination that has kept them unbeaten in 28 normal-time games since March 2024.
team_style_away: Austria under Rangnick use a high-intensity 4-2-3-1 centered on aggressive gegenpressing, rapid transitions, and relentless pressing to win the ball high up the pitch.
wc_history_home: Spain won their first and only World Cup title in 2010 in South Africa, defeating the Netherlands 1-0 in extra time, becoming the first European team to win the tournament outside of Europe.
wc_history_away: Austria's best World Cup finish was third place in 1954 (including a historic 7-5 win over Switzerland), and they return to the tournament in 2026 for the first time since 1998 after a 28-year absence.
group_context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
strengths_home: Lamine Yamal's exceptional 1v1 dribbling and creativity on the right flank
Dominant midfield control and ball circulation through Rodri and Pedri
28-game unbeaten run in normal time reflecting squad depth and tactical cohesion
strengths_away: Rangnick's organised gegenpressing system disrupts opponents' build-up effectively
Deep squad with Bundesliga quality throughout midfield
Excellent recent form — four wins from last five with only two goals conceded
